title: zornia tenuifolia
intro: Zornia tenuifolia, also known as Running Buffalo Clover, is a perennial legume native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and narrow leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and saponins. The health benefits of Zornia tenuifolia include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and its dried leaves and flowers can be used in herbal remedies, making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural health practitioners.
